violations.description: General employee cleanliness. — --Food employee did not wash hands as necessary. -Employee at Stockman's Club did not wash hands between glove changes. -H/W sink at Stockman's and the Carvery were dry upon beginning of inspection.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. -Remove cardboard on dry storage racks next to freezer.
Temperature measuring devices. — --Temperature measuring device was not provided. -Need probe thermometer that reads 0-220F at the Rock County Smokehouse mobile stand.
General equipment and utensils - Maintenance and operation. — --Nonfood contact equipment/utensil was not maintained in a good repair or sound condition. -Defrost Dippin Dots freezer.

Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was not provided in sufficient number and/or not accessible to allow convenient use by employees. -Need at Rock County Smokehouse stand. Also need paper towels and hand soap. -Need paper towels at Stockman's Club handwash.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…)were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. -Need to clean floor drain below dishwash machine in kitchen.

Food display and service. — --Potentially hazardous food was not kept at an internal temperature of 140F or above during display or service. -Steam table at the Rock County Smokehouse upstairs holding hot dogs was at 100F. Need to add more water.--Potentially hazardous food was not kept at an internal temperature of 41F or below during display or service. -Cheese and mayonnaise cooler at the Carvery was at 59F. (adjusted temperature control-was set on warm)
